Overview

Address
5270 East Los Angeles Avenue, Simi Valley, CA 93063
Phone
8055229155
Certified beds
99
Avg daily residents
93 (94% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit LLC
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1997-05-21
Setting
Urban
3 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 3/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.77 5.44 3.39 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.78 0.95 0.55 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.38 1.21 0.98 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.62 3.28 1.86 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 2.15 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.08 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
4.38
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.63
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.11
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
43.3%
annual
RN turnover
70.6%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.93
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.40
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-08-07 26 19 7 168 1 168
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-07-26 22 12 11 92 1 92
Total weighted health score
149.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (57)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

56 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2026-01-22 2026-02-02
0561 Honor the resident's right to and the facility must promote and facilitate resident self-determination through support of resident choice. D 2025-08-07 2025-09-05
0605 Prevent the use of unnecessary psychotropic medications or use medications that may restrain a resident's ability to function. D 2025-08-07 2025-09-05
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-08-07 2025-09-05
0644 Coordinate assessments with the pre-admission screening and resident review program; and referring for services as needed. D 2025-08-07 2025-09-05
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-08-07 2025-09-05
0685 Assist a resident in gaining access to vision and hearing services. D 2025-08-07 2025-09-05
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-08-07 2025-09-05
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2025-08-07 2025-09-05
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. E 2025-08-07 2025-09-05
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2025-08-07 2025-09-05
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. F 2025-08-07 2025-09-05
0802 Provide sufficient support personnel to safely and effectively carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service. D 2025-08-07 2025-09-05
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. D 2025-08-07 2025-09-05
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. D 2025-08-07 2025-09-05
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2025-08-07 2025-09-05
0825 Provide or get specialized rehabilitative services as required for a resident. D 2025-08-07 2025-09-05
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-08-07 2025-09-05
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-08-07 2025-09-05
0919 Make sure that a working call system is available in each resident's bathroom and bathing area. D 2025-08-07 2025-09-05
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. G 2025-06-30 2025-07-28
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. E 2025-06-30 2025-07-28
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. E 2025-06-30 2025-07-28
0940 Develop, implement, and/or maintain an effective training program for all new and existing staff members. D 2025-06-30 2025-07-28
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-04-16 2025-05-15
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-03-17 2025-04-01
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2025-01-25 2025-03-17
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2025-01-25 2025-03-18
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-01-06 2025-02-10
0919 Make sure that a working call system is available in each resident's bathroom and bathing area. D 2024-09-03 2024-09-05
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2024-07-26 2024-07-29
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. D 2024-07-26 2024-08-16
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-07-26 2024-08-16
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2024-07-26 2024-08-16
0711 Ensure the resident's doctor reviews the resident's care, writes, signs and dates progress notes and orders, at each required visit. D 2024-07-26 2024-08-16
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2024-07-26 2024-08-16
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. D 2024-07-26 2024-08-16
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2024-07-26 2024-08-16
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-07-26 2024-08-16
0887 Educate residents and staff on COVID-19 vaccination, offer the COVID-19 vaccine to eligible residents and staff after education, and properly document each resident and staff member's vaccination status. D 2024-07-26 2024-08-16
0919 Make sure that a working call system is available in each resident's bathroom and bathing area. D 2024-07-26 2024-08-16
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. D 2024-07-26 2024-08-16
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2024-07-26 2024-08-16
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-06-04 2024-06-20
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2024-06-04 2024-06-20
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-06-04 2024-06-20
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-04-02 2024-04-02
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-02-07 2024-02-23
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2021-12-09 2021-12-29
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2021-12-09 2021-12-29

Methodology & sources
  • Nursing Home Provider Info (nh-provider-info), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,703 rows.
  • Nursing Home Penalties (nh-penalties), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 16,915 rows.
  • Nursing Home Health Deficiencies (nh-deficiencies), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 418,972 rows.
  • Nursing Home Ownership (nh-ownership), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 160,393 rows.
  • Skilled Nursing Facility Cost Report (snf-cost-report), vintage 2022, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,120 rows.

Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description
